evidence: The majority of this demand is driven by the power needs and projected power needs of data centers to serve an increasingly digital economy and to support artificial intelligence.
Extending service to these customers necessitates significant capital expenditures, which in turn requires sufficient access to sources of capital.

The traditional electric operating companies are experiencing projected demand that significantly exceeds recent experience, creating the need for new power generating resources and transmission facilities.
The majority of this demand is driven by the power needs and projected power needs of data centers to serve an increasingly digital economy and to support artificial intelligence.
Use of generative artificial intelligence has also increased the frequency, scale, and sophistication of cyber attacks and increased the capability of less sophisticated actors.
As generative artificial intelligence continues to evolve, new technologies and increased computing power could cause these trends to continue or exacerbate.

The increased concentration of business within a single industry based on emerging technologies, and uncertainties on the actual capacity required to satisfy the projected new demands of these new industries creates risks.

capex_mentions.quote: Extending service to these customers necessitates significant capital expenditures
capex_mentions.context: Capital expenditures required to serve data center and AI-driven load growth; no dollar amount specified

limitations: ai_investment_intensity: Southern Company is a utility, not an AI builder; AI-attributable capex is framed as infrastructure to serve AI-driven customer demand (data centers), not direct AI R&D or AI product investment. No dollar amounts are disclosed in the excerpts, limiting score to 3.

ai_narrative_concreteness: AI is discussed primarily as a demand driver for the utility's grid/generation build-out rather than as a deployed internal use case; no quantified results or deployment metrics provided.

ai_risk_disclosure_depth: Risk-section and MD&A material is present; AI risks are named with specific mechanisms (cyber sophistication, load concentration, geopolitical escalation) but no quantification or named governance/oversight bodies are disclosed, capping score at 4.
Excerpts are limited; additional AI-related disclosures may exist elsewhere in the full filing.
